full_prompt: Design the primary structure of a 50 kg class microsatellite for a student-demonstration microsatellite slot. The envelope is the published 50 kg class volume for the microsatellite program (approximately 500 mm x 500 mm x 500 mm with attachment ring); the total wet mass must not exceed 50 kg. The launch environment is defined per the program launch vehicle: random vibration 14.1 Grms (NASA GEVS qualification-style spectrum, 20-2000 Hz), and shock response spectrum 1000 G peak in the 1000-10000 Hz band at the separation plane. A quasi-static limit load of 20 g axial and 10 g lateral is applied at the spacecraft CG. The primary structure must carry and survive all three environments: quasi-static, random vibration, and pyroshock. Natural-frequency requirement: the first longitudinal mode must be above 110 Hz and the first lateral mode must be above 45 Hz, consistent with the launch vehicle decoupling requirement. Stress allowables follow NASA-STD-5001B: yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4. Material is typically Al 7075-T7351 or Al 6061-T6 for the primary structure. Primary verification is structural FEA (linear static for quasi-static; random-vibration harmonic response with 3-sigma stress evaluation; modal analysis for first-mode frequency). Vibration analysis is a primary gate here, not secondary. Thermal and fluid analyses are explicitly NOT required at this specific structural-qualification gate (thermal survival is verified at a separate thermal gate). Radiation analysis is explicitly NOT required since the student demo orbit is LEO. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the primary structure, a mass budget, and a report listing wet mass in kg, first longitudinal mode in Hz, first lateral mode in Hz, max quasi-static stress in MPa with FoS, 3-sigma random-vibration peak stress in MPa, and shock-response displacement at critical mounting interfaces.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structure of a 50 kg-class microsatellite for a student-demonstration microsatellite slot. Envelope approximately 500 x 500 x 500 mm including the attachment ring; wet mass <= 50 kg. Stress allowables per NASA-STD-5001B: yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4. Primary structure typically Al 7075-T7351 or Al 6061-T6.

Load cases at the adapter-ring separation plane.
- LC1 quasi-static 20 g axial limit load at the spacecraft CG.
- LC2 quasi-static 10 g lateral limit load at the spacecraft CG.
- LC3 random vibration 14.1 Grms over 20-2000 Hz (NASA GEVS-style qualification spectrum).
- LC4 pyroshock SRS: 1000 G peak over 1000-10000 Hz.
- First longitudinal mode >= 110 Hz and first lateral mode >= 45 Hz (launch-vehicle decoupling).

prompt.title: 50 kg-class microsatellite primary structure
prompt.brief: Design a 50 kg-class microsatellite primary structure that survives 14.1 Grms random vib, 1000 G shock SRS, 20 g axial quasi-static, first modes ≥ 110 Hz long / ≥ 45 Hz lat.

exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: LEO demo, not in student-slot structural gate.
